items.description: Wall Street is kicking off the second half of 2026 with fresh conviction: shrugging off one shock after another, markets are set to grind even higher.

items.title: Brown University’s Rating Outlook Lowered to Negative by Moody’s
items.description: Brown University had the outlook on its credit rating lowered to negative on Thursday by Moody’s Ratings, which cited expense pressures facing the institution.

items.title: Barclays Pulls Near $1 Billion Debt Deal for Sound Physicians
items.description: Barclays Plc has withdrawn a near $1 billion financing for Sound Inpatient Physicians Inc. from the debt capital markets, marking one of the few leveraged loan deals to be pulled this year.

items.title: Invesco’s Brill Gives SpaceX Bond a Red Card for ‘Very Sloppy’ Performance
items.description: Invesco Advisers Inc.’s head of investment-grade credit for North America slammed SpaceX’s inaugural bond sale on Thursday for a “very sloppy” secondary market performance.

items.title: JPMorgan Loses Battle Over Charlie Javice’s Legal Expenses
items.description: A Delaware judge said JPMorgan Chase & Co. failed to show that Charlie Javice‘s $74 million in legal fees were clearly excessive, ruling the bank must continue to pay her bills.

items.title: Genuine Parts Is Said to Receive O’Reilly Interest for Auto Unit
items.description: Genuine Parts Co. has attracted a cash bid for its auto-parts arm from O’Reilly Automotive Inc. as it seeks to refocus on its industrials business, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Screwworm's Return to the US Sets up Yearslong Fight
items.description: For the first time in about fifty years, the New World screwworm is back in US livestock. Bloomberg's Ilena Peng explains how the parasitic fly will affect beef prices and how the USDA is responding.

items.title: Trump Financial Disclosure Shows 21,000 Trades in 2025
items.description: President Donald Trump made more than 21,000 securities trades in his first year back in office, often in intense bursts tied to market events he created.
